desc:
You find yourself amid a stand of thorny bushes which tear your clothes and skin
as you try to wade through it. It is at times so thick and dense that you wonder
whether you will become stuck in it. But, fortunately, your constant pain pays
off as your limbs move you, albeit slow and painfully, through this sea of
thorns.
name: In the Brush

desc:
You find yourself amid a dense stand of thorny bushes, which pluck and tear at
you as you try to move through. It is at times so thick and dense that it seems
you could soon become completely stuck. But fortunately things have not yet
become quite so bad.
name: The Flats

desc:
You find yourself amid a stand of thorny bushes which tear your clothes and skin
as you try to wade through it. It is at times so thick and dense that you wonder
whether you will become stuck in it. But, fortunately, your constant pain pays
off as your limbs move you, albeit slow and painfully, through this sea of
thorns.
name: Scrubland

desc:
Jagged and broken rocks which have peeled away from the crags to the north and
fallen here clutter these stony and infertile plains. The ground is virtually
bare of vegetation, and rains have carved deep washes and ravines in the
gravelly soil.
name: Broken Plains

desc:
The hard and stony ground here lies slightly higher than the surrounding plains
to the south and west. Northward, the land rises gently up the flank of a hill.
Far off upon the summit of the hill, a foreboding tower stands guard over the
land. A few hardy shrubs cling to life in the thin soil but no wildlife can be
seen or heard. Silence blankets the land.
name: High Plains

desc:
You are standing at the beginning of some broken plains. While the land is
gentle to the west and south, the land to the east and north is harsh. Off to
the northeast, the broken plains rise up into cliff-like hills. There is a
gravel-strewn slope leading down to the east.
name: Broken Plains

desc:
Knee deep in grass, you pause to survey your surroundings. To the south and
west, the rolling plains continue. Northwards is the Old East Road, beyond
which, the plains continue. To the east, however, the gently rolling grasslands
give way to harsher, rockier plains.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
Like the swells of the ocean, the land around you rises and falls, covered not
by foam but tall grass. Also, like the sea, not a soul can be seen. All around
you, the rolling plains continue. In the distance to the east, you can see the
land rise up into steep, rocky hills.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
You are standing in an area of gently rolling plains. The ground here is covered
with tough, dry grass. All around you is silence, as if nature were afraid to
make its presence known. The plains continue to the east, while to the north and
west the grass is replaced by tall, thorny bushes. Off in the distance to the
east, you can make out sharp, jagged hills.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
You are standing upon one of many plots of dry, broken earth which make up these
plains. Around you grow evil looking shrubs and weeds that grow taller than
you'd think possible. Small shoots of brown grass try to grow within the cracks
of the dried earth which creaks like old floorboards when you walk on it. These
flats stretch away to the south, bordered to the east by a band of shrubs, and
giving way to rolling plains to the north and east.
name: The Flats

desc:
You are standing among some gently rolling plains covered with tough, waist-
high grass. An eerie silence blankets the land, making you feel like an
unwelcome visitor. To the south and west, the land flattens out into dry
flatlands. The rolling plains continue to the north and east, but you can see
some jagged hills rising up to the northeast.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
You had never thought that grasslands could be so hot. Without shade, the sun
can beat down on you without respite. All around you, these gently rolling
plains continue, except in the distance to the northeast where you can see where
the land rises up and becomes much rockier.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
Except for the swish of your legs through the grass and the occasional buzz of
an insect, the silence of this land is unbroken. You get the impression that
people rarely travel this land. Judging from the uneasy feeling you get from the
broken lands to the north and east, you think you understand why. Switching your
attention to the south and east, where the grasslands continue, the feeling
passes.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
You are at the edge of the Lone-lands proper, where the land becomes rugged and
inhospitable. To the west and south, the land smoothes out into gently rolling
plains. To the north and east, the land becomes more broken. Cliff-like hills
dominate the skyline to the north and northeast. East, you see a thicket, a
veritable garden amid this harsh dry land.
name: Broken Plains

desc:
You are in the middle of a dense thicket. Around you is the tortured land of the
Lone-lands. To the north is a steep cliff wall, while to the west and east the
broken plains continue. The plains also continue to the south, but a combination
of roots, gravel, and the rocks make it impossible to go that way.
name: A Dense Thicket

desc:
You seem to have come to the end of the gently rolling plains. Off to the north
and east, the land becomes rocky and dry. Further to the northeast, the land
actually rises up into rocky hills that give you an unexpected feeling of dread.
To the west, the grasslands continue.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
The silence of these lands has become almost oppressive. Looking around for
relief from the monotony of these grasslands, your eyes fall upon the rocky
hills to the northeast. A shiver going down your spine, you start to wonder if
monotony doesn't have its advantages. You can continue through these plains to
the west, north, and east.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
The gently rolling grasslands of this part of the Lone-lands surround you. While
you might hear the occasional buzz of an insect or rustle of the wind in the
grass, the land seems empty and forgotten. The grasslands continue to the north
and east while, to the west, the land turns into dry flats. Off in the distance,
to the northeast, the land rises up into cliff-like hills.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
You are standing upon one of many plots of dry, broken earth which make up these
plains. Around you grow evil looking shrubs and weeds that grow taller than
you'd think possible. Small shoots of brown grass try to grow within the cracks
of the dried earth which creaks like old floorboards when you walk on it. These
flats stretch away to the west, giving way to rolling plains to the north and
east.
name: The Flats

desc:
You are standing upon one of many plots of dry, broken earth which make up these
plains. Around you grow evil looking shrubs and weeds that grow taller than
you'd think possible. Small shoots of brown grass try to grow within the cracks
of the dried earth which creaks like old floorboards when you walk on it. These
flats stretch away to the north and east, bordered to the west by a band of
shrubs.
name: The Flats

desc:
Yellowed clumps of grass and lichen provide partial cover to the grey rock and
stone of the hillside. The land rises to hills in the north and slopes downward
to the broken plains to the south. Looking around, a few small gullies break up
the otherwise featureless plains. Several miles to the north, a dark and
blackened tower stands silently overlooking the plains.
name: Bleak Grassland

desc:
To the north, east and the south, the ground drops gradually away to the plains
below. The hillside spur here provides an excellent vantage point from which you
can see far out over the surrounding lands. Equally the open terrain provides
little or no concealment and leaves you visible to any keen-eyed creature abroad
in these desolate plains. Several miles to the north-west, a tower stares down
upon you from the crest of a hill.
name: Raised Plains

desc:
You are in the middle of a dense thicket. You haven't seen much in the way of
vegetation in the Lone-lands, except for the dry, tough grass that covers the
ground. There are a variety of plants in this thicket, some that don't even look
familiar. Peering out of the relative safety of the thicket, you see the start
of a gully to the south and the continuation of the broken plains to the north
and east. To the west is a high, rocky outcropping.
name: A Dense Thicket

desc:
You have entered a burial crypt. While it appears to have been hastily
constructed, you can tell that it is of Dunedain, if not Numenorean, make. All
about this dark chamber are carvings in the walls and on the raised dais upon
which rests a stone sarcophagus. The presence of Evil hangs in the air, trying
to suffocate all that is living.
name: A Burial Crypt

desc:
Finding your way in this rocky land has proven difficult. To the south, you way
is blocked by a rocky outcropping, but you can make out paths to the north and
east. To the west, a thicket promises to make travel difficult, but perhaps it
could provide an place to rest in relative safety. Beyond the thicket, the land
rises up into cliff-like hills. A sickening presence to the northwest makes your
stomach knot with fear.
name: Broken Plains

desc:
The Lone-land plains stretch out far and flat to the north, south and west. To
the east a river has carved a steep-sided ravine into the land. As you look over
the edge, water sparkles and dances its way southward along the base of the
canyon. Though barely audible, the tinkling sound of running water reaches up to
your ears, breaking the lonely silence of the plains.
name: Above a River Canyon

desc:
A smattering of hardy grasses grows here on the cracked plains. To the north,
south and west the plains continue toward the horizon. Immediately to the east,
cracks in the ground grow in size where a steep-sided cliff falls away. Looking
over the edge, you can make out a small river undercutting the high ground from
which you now gaze. The entire cliff edge here looks unstable and liable to fall
at any minute.
name: Above the River

desc:
Exposed and bleak, the terrain stretches out around you. Few trees or bushes can
survive on this thin and infertile ground. Even the scattered tussocks of grass
and heather which grow here look brown and weathered. Several miles to the
north-west, the plains give way to an area of low hills. Broken plains meet the
eye to all the remaining compass points.
name: Irregular Ground

desc:
Exposed and windswept, the terrain here provides little comfort or shelter. Here
and there a lichen-covered boulder lies clear of the hard, featureless ground.
Scrub and short grasses grow where they can, though there is little soil on
which they can gain purchase. The land drops slightly to the north and the west
into one of the undulations which break up the landscape.
name: Desolate Plains

desc:
The flat and sterile terrain is broken up by numerous steep-walled washes
deepened by wind and rain. Travel is difficult, and little seems to live here. A
few tough and prickly leaves, thistles and nettles, poke from between the cracks
in the stony soil.
name: Broken Plains

desc:
You pause a moment to wipe a pasty combination of sweat, dust and grime from
your forehead and survey your surroundings. You are in the middle of some broken
terrain. You could call it plains, if it wasn't so rocky. Just to the north,
passage is impeded by a rocky outcropping that is the beginning of some hills.
To the east, the plains continue between two outcroppings, while to the south,
the gravel-covered ground slopes away. To the west of you is a thicket promising
shade and rest within.
name: Broken Plains

desc:
You are standing in between two rocky outcroppings that form a kind of gully.
The rocks provide a little shade, but they don't alleviate the dryness of these
lands. In fact, their baked surfaces only imprint it more firmly in your mind.
The gully continues to the east, while to the west, the terrain opens up a
little bit.
name: Broken Plains

desc:
You pause to catch your breath and wipe your brow in the shade these rocky
outcroppings provide. Looking around, you see that the rocks form a little gully
here. To the north, where the terrain opens up, there is a dense thicket that is
sure to provide even more shade. The gully continues to the west.
name: Broken Plains

desc:
This seems to be the dim head of a gully plunging down to the north. The walls
of the gulch are a hundred feet high, and the sky is only a narrow slash high
above; the direct rays of the sun never fall here. A dismal grey-green moss
covers some of the dimly lit rocks. To the south is a dark cave-mouth from which
the vanished stream once poured; a faint and musty breeze wafts from it.
name: A Rocky Gully

desc:
The cave is much bigger than it appeared to be from the outside. It has smooth
stone walls, probably from the water that once flowed through on its course to
the valley. A path leads east, but it is too dark to tell what lies in that
direction.
name: Cave in the Hillside

desc:
Here the breeze blows steadily toward the west, to what looks to be a small
cavern. Around a slight bend, you see rays of light. It appears to be natural
light, from the sun or moon, but you cannot be sure. Getting out of the twists
and turns of the passages behind you is a very welcome thought.
name: A Hollow Ridge

desc:
Moving on, you soon reach another nexus. Tunnels trail off in all directions
from this place. All details of what might lie ahead are consumed by the
oppressive darkness. A faint scent of urine and excrement tells you that
something dwells inside these corridors of stone.
name: A Hollow Ridge

desc:
You are at a four-way junction now. In every direction lie more of the same
rocky passages. You try to remember which way is out, but each tunnel looks the
same. Using the directional flow of the wind does not seem to work, as the faint
breeze seems to be coming from every tunnel.
name: A Hollow Ridge

desc:
The ridge splits three ways here. To the north and west, it joins more passages
like the one you are in now. To the south however, it looks as though the tunnel
dead-ends at a small cavern. The earth in here smells dry and old, but fresh air
keeps the place well ventilated.
name: A Hollow Ridge

desc:
Dipping slightly below the pool, is a hollowed ridge that once served as an
aqueduct to bring water to the pool. It is bone dry now though, as the water has
chosen alternate means to get to its destination. The air is fresh here, cool
and dry. For the freshness, the tunnel must have another exit, shall you bravely
attempt to find it?
name: A Hollowed-out Ridge

desc:
Behind the tall rocks you have discovered a small pool of refreshing water.
Sheltered on all sides by the relatively large boulders, you realise how it can
exist in such a hot, dry landscape as it is comfortably shaded. Looking over the
rocks to the west, you guess that this was once the site of a nice waterfall
that cascaded down to the valley below. A smooth line of rocks, out of place in
the jagged cliffs, marks the water's descent. But where did this water come
from?
name: A Hidden Pool

desc:
Before you is a small depression that once was probably a nice watering hole. A
sinking feeling overcomes you as you realise that it is totally dry now. The
only derivation of moisture comes from a thin sheen of dew on one of the rocks
at the north side of the basin. Any liquid that might escape however, is quickly
sucked up by the dry earth.
name: A Small Levee

desc:
This broad, flat plateau extends for miles to the east and north (where the
ground seems higher). The ground is stony and the vegetation is sparse - coarse
grass, nettles, an occasional thorny bush. Nothing grows here that would be
edible by respectable folk. It must rain here often enough, judging from the
water-carved gullies here and there, but the water must drain quickly into the
permeable stone. To the west there appears to be a fold or break in the
otherwise seamless grey landscape.
name: A Wide Plateau

desc:
This is a broad shelf on the northwest face of a rocky hillside that rises to
the south. The rock is flat and soil-less, and only a few scraggly bushes grow
here. A fitful breeze blows the dust of the Lone-Lands across the stony surface.
This location gives a good view of the ground far below to the north and west.
name: Ledge Overlooking a Settlement

desc:
The stony ground continues for miles in all directions - flattish, but covered
with broken and worn boulders and fragments of rock. Brownish grass and weeds
scrape out a miserable living in the crevices and around the intermittent rain
puddles.
name: Stony Plateau

desc:
The ground is flattish, the horizon is distant and unmarked by hills, but the
uneven and stone-covered ground makes travel unpleasant and difficult for foot,
hoof, or paw. Coarse-leafed thorny bushes huddle in brownish clumps. The quiet
is almost absolute, with only an occasional slithering rustle from behind a
stone.
name: Stony Plateau

desc:
The ground here climbs toward the west in a maze of water- and wind-carved rocky
outcroppings, pinnacles, blocks, and gullies. The going is difficult and the way
hard to find. If there was ever a trail through this jumble, it has long since
disappeared.
name: Rocky Slope

desc:
Carved crudely out of the stone, this staircase descends precipitously. The cut
stone is not smooth and shapely like dwarven work. Rather it is rough cut and
functional. Whatever the case, it is not here by natural means, which only
leaves one question; that of who built this.
name: A Stone Staircase

desc:
Traversing the narrow stair, you are careful with your movements. The bottom of
the passage is still not visible, and it might be a long way to the landing.
Glancing at the stone walls, you notice no artwork or other tell-tale signs as
to the origin or function of this place. A quick check of your light source is
comforting, as it is pitch-dark.
name: A Stone Staircase

desc:
You seem to be at the bottom of the large cliff. A narrow stone staircase leads
upward further than you can see. The floor here is hard dirt, and the ceiling is
relatively high. There seems to be a massive slab of rock blocking travel to the
west, leaving only back up the stair as an obvious option.
name: A Stone Staircase

desc:
Emerging from the bottom of the cliff, this gate is not easily visible and in a
place that would be easy to defend. The gate itself is two stone slabs placed
together in a formation that would make opening from the other side all but
impossible. Around you is the fertile beauty of the valley, green and lush. The
river gurgles lazily in the background.
name: Gate to the Valley

desc:
Standing in front of the gate, you spy buildings and pathways that indicate a
small village. You wonder who would locate their homes here, deep inside the
valley. They must be a small race of people, as the homes and other structures
around you are smaller than elven-kind. From somewhere inside the village you
hear excited shouting, perhaps the villagers have spotted your arrival. Will you
be welcomed?
name: Entrance to the Village

desc:
Proceeding into the heart of the village, you find yourself on a dirt track
leading toward the small river. On either side are small dwellings used by the
citizens of the village for various purposes. Glancing around, you try to
discern the magnitude of the village. Spying no tall spires or towers, you guess
that this is pretty much the extent of it. Alleyways branch off leading to small
houses, but no other roads.
name: Path through the Village

desc:
Still proceeding east-west, you surmise that this is the main road of the
village. As you explore, you notice that none of the citizens are anxious to
approach you. No nods of greeting, no friendly waves. If this is the nature of
all its citizens, then it's small wonder that the village is so secluded.
Unfriendly as they seem to be, no outward signs of hostility are visible either.
name: Path through the Village

desc:
This seems to be the museum of the village, containing various historical
artefacts and memorabilia. Books line shelves, perhaps if you glanced at some,
you could learn more about this village and its people.
name: A Small Mathom-House

desc:
The basement of the mathom is dark and musty. Moisture seeps in through cracks
in the walls and makes the air heavy around you. This place is used as a dump
for unwanted supplies and relics. A few broken weapons and worn out armour
litter the floor.
name: Cellar

desc:
Small houses with wooden walls and thatch roofs line the path. From small
windows you catch glimpses of curious hobbits peering out at you. In the yards
of others, frightened mothers usher their children into the safety of the
buildings. You wonder about the frightened looks, and figure that these folk
have some great tragedy of the past looming over them. Perhaps the house to your
east can provide more answers, it looks different than the other homes.
name: Stoor Homes

desc:
This seems to be the place where the Stoors gather for town meetings and other
social events. It is small, but well suited for the populace of the Stoor
community. You see fisherman swapping stories of monster sized fish that got
away, and children laughing as they play.
name: A Quiet Park

desc:
The well-trodden dirt path runs east-west through the bulk of the village. Small
alleys branch off into small homes and down to the river edge, but this seems to
be the only major thoroughfare. Citizens stare at you in wide-eyed wonder as if
you are the first outsider they have seen.
name: Path through the Village

desc:
The dirt path turns into a nice cobblestone walkway, obviously leading to the
house to the west. The house is not overly large, yet is much larger than the
other structures in the village. It looks well kept and the doormen in front of
the door imply that something important is inside.
name: A Stone Walk

desc:
The foyer is large, seemingly able to hold the entire village populace at one
time. On the walls are paintings of men and women attired in fancy halfling
clothes. The floor is wooden and seems to be well taken care of. The only
feature that catches you eye however, is the set of double oaken doors to your
north.
name: An Important Looking House

desc:
A simple office, one that defies the marvel of the rest of the house. The
official that works here must mean business. Spartan furnishings scatter the
office only for functional measure. A few wooden chairs, a wastebasket, a pair
of lamps. The only elegant feature of the place is the massive desk scattered
with papers. The desk is from a rich wood, and highly polished.
name: The Village Chief's Office

desc:
A simple shop, apparently here to serve the needs of the village fishermen. A
few nice boats are being repaired, and a raft lies unused in the corner. Located
by the bend of the river, it provides easy access to either direction. West,
towards the mouth of the river, and south, leading eventually to the Hoarwell
and beyond.
name: The Boat House

desc:
In the river, you get a chance to look up at the mighty canyon walls that tower
over you. Their presence gives you a certain feeling of security and solidness.
It is this feature of the land, you guess, that made the townsfolk pick this
site for a village.
name: A Small River

desc:
The shallow river winds generally south and west through a gorge sixty feet
deep. The cliffs rise sheerly, striated in shades of beige, grey, red-brown and
gold. The water is clear and cool, and the shapes of fish can be seen darting
against the coloured pebbles on the river bottom.
name: A Small River

desc:
The river winds into a series of curves here, slowly making its way to the
Hoarwell and beyond. Rocks jut up from the bottom of the river bed at this
point, making boat-travel an unwise choice. The fish swimming in the water look
like a tantalizing lunch, or snack.
name: A Small River

desc:
The water of the river is clear enough to see the bottom. Fish swim by
peacefully, avoiding the lures of the fishermen. Green grass lines the sides of
the river, making several nice places to stop and have a spot of lunch. The
river is relatively narrow and shallow, but obviously big enough to supply the
village with fresh food to eat.
name: A Small River

desc:
This is where the river originates, sprouting from the ground as it escapes the
many miles of underground tunnels it must have passed through on its way here.
The water is cold, having come from the mountains of the Weather Hills in the
north-west. To the south, the river winds its way to join the Hoarwell, and
eventually to end up in the waters of the River Bruinen.
name: Source of the River

desc:
You are standing at the beginning of the dry, rugged lands known as the
Lone-lands. The lands truly seem lonely, as even the normal animal sounds you
normally hear have disappeared. The much more pleasant rolling plains lie to the
west while the rougher terrain continues to the east. A small cave entrance can
be seen to the north at the foot of a low ridge of jagged hills.
name: Broken Plains

desc:
Uneven rock forms the walls and ceiling of this cavity beneath the hills. Once
an unspoiled creation of nature, it has now been claimed as a den by some
creature. Dry, flattened grass lies in one corner of the cave, and on the
opposite side there are several bones littering the earthen floor.
name: In a Small, Dry Den

desc:
This small dwelling is obviously used as the gate-guards' resting place. There
are racks on the walls holding small shields and swords. A few pieces of leather
armour hang on nearby hooks. Some small shelves hold daily food supplies and a
small book or two.
name: Gate-watchers' Hut
